# indelible

> Detect when your AI agent silently drifts. indelible captures a signed
> behavioral fingerprint (refusal rate, latency, vocab entropy, tool-use
> distribution) and re-runs it on demand — your CI gate for model-update day.

## What problem this solves

Model providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Together) silently update their
models. Your agent works today; tomorrow, after a quiet RLHF adjustment, it
might refuse more requests, use tools differently, or talk noticeably terser.
You usually find out when a customer notices first.

indelible captures a **signed JSON fingerprint** of your agent's behavior
on a fixed test set. On any later day — pre-deploy, scheduled CI, after a
provider's announcement — `indelible verify` re-runs the same suite and tells
you, signal by signal, what changed. Result: `pass` / `warn` / `breach`,
exit codes 0 / 1 / 2.

It is **offline-verifiable** (the signature lives in a file, not a service)
and **cross-provider** (attest on Anthropic, verify on Groq if you want).
The output is a single JSON file: diffable, signable, auditable.

## Key concepts

- **Fingerprint**: JSON file capturing 7 behavioral signals from a test suite run.
- **Attestation**: the process of running test inputs, collecting signals, signing the result.
- **Verification**: re-running the same suite and comparing signals tolerance-aware.
- **Signal**: a single behavioral measurement (`name`, `value`, optional `digest`, per-signal `tolerance`).
- **`config_hash`**: SHA-256 of the canonical agent config — guards against verifying with a different system_prompt/tools.
- **`test_set_hash`**: SHA-256 of the prompt list — guards against verifying with a different test set.

## CLI

```
indelible init                       # scaffold key + indelible.toml + prompts.json
indelible attest                     # produces indelible.fingerprint.json + .sig + .sig.pub
indelible verify                     # exit 0=pass 1=warn 2=breach 3=error
indelible diff a.json b.json         # diff two fingerprints offline (no API calls)

# Multi-agent repos: attest each agent into its own fingerprint
indelible attest --config agents/coding.toml --out fingerprints/coding.json
indelible verify --config agents/coding.toml --fp fingerprints/coding.json
```

## Python API

```python
from indelible.attest import attest
from indelible.verify import verify
from indelible.config import IndelibleConfig

config = IndelibleConfig.from_toml("indelible.toml")
fp = attest(config, test_inputs=[...], model="claude-opus-4-7",
            sign_key="~/.indelible/key.pem",
            sig_out_path="indelible.fingerprint.json.sig")

import json
with open("indelible.fingerprint.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(fp.to_dict(), f, indent=2)

report = verify("indelible.fingerprint.json", config, "claude-opus-4-7",
                test_inputs=[...], sig_path="indelible.fingerprint.json.sig")
if report.breached:
    raise RuntimeError(report.summary())
```

## Signals

| Signal | Default tolerance | Meaning |
|---|---:|---|
| `refusal_rate` | 0.10 (±10pp) | fraction of outputs matching refusal patterns |
| `latency` | 0.30 (±300ms) | P50/P95/mean response time in seconds |
| `vocab_entropy` | 0.50 (bits) | Shannon entropy of output vocabulary |
| `tool_distribution` | 0.10 | histogram of tool-call frequencies |
| `tool_schema_hash` | 0.00 (exact) | SHA-256 of canonicalised tool schemas — uses `digest` field, not numeric tolerance |
| `embedding_profile` | 0.05 (cosine) | mean cosine similarity to output centroid (needs `[deep]` extra) |
| `anchor_drift` | 0.05 (cosine) | cosine distance from system-prompt anchor (needs `[deep]` extra) |

Defaults documented in `docs/TOLERANCES.md`. Each Signal can override its own
tolerance; collectors set sensible per-signal values, the dataclass default
(0.05) applies only to hand-built Signals.

## Config format (indelible.toml)

```toml
[agent]
name = "my-agent"
system_prompt = "You are a helpful assistant."
model = "claude-opus-4-7"
provider_url = "https://api.anthropic.com"
tolerance_default = 0.05
maintainer = "alice@example.com"   # signed into every fingerprint

# Optional — override built-in English+German refusal patterns:
[refusal]
patterns = [
    "I cannot",
    "I'm not able to",
    "ne peux pas",     # French
    "no puedo",        # Spanish
]

[[tools]]
name = "search"
description = "Search the web"
```

## How attest + verify work end-to-end

1. `indelible init` generates an Ed25519 key at `~/.indelible/key.pem` and scaffolds `indelible.toml` + `prompts.json`.
2. `indelible attest` reads the config, runs each prompt against the model, runs 7 collectors over the responses, builds a `Fingerprint` dataclass, signs `Fingerprint.canonical_bytes()` (sorted-keys JSON), writes:
   - `indelible.fingerprint.json` — the fingerprint
   - `indelible.fingerprint.json.sig` — Ed25519 signature
   - `indelible.fingerprint.json.sig.pub` — companion public key (so verify is self-contained)
3. `indelible verify` loads the fingerprint, validates the signature against the companion `.pub`, then re-runs attest on the current model and compares signal by signal. Context guards short-circuit early on `config_hash` or `test_set_hash` mismatch — saves the API budget for runs whose verdict is already breach.

## When to use indelible

- After model upgrades — verify behavior hasn't regressed before promoting to prod.
- CI gate — fail the build if behavioral contract is breached (exit code 2).
- EU AI Act baseline — fingerprints are "documented behavioral baselines."
- Multi-provider comparison — same prompts, two providers, signed diff.

## When NOT to use indelible

- Real-time observability — use Langfuse, Helicone, or Arize Phoenix.
- Quality / correctness testing — use Promptfoo or DeepEval. indelible checks distributions, not correctness.
- Detecting bugs in your own code — that's what unit tests are for.
- Detecting jailbreak / prompt-injection susceptibility — use Garak (NVIDIA) or PyRIT (Microsoft).

## FAQ

### How is this different from Promptfoo?
Promptfoo is an eval framework: it asks "is this output correct on this prompt?" indelible asks "did the *distribution* of behavior change since last week?" They are complements: use Promptfoo for the quality dimension, indelible for behavioral consistency. Some teams gate CI on both.

### Does it support Claude / OpenAI / Ollama / Groq?
All four. Provider is auto-detected from the model string:
- `claude-*` or `anthropic/*` → Anthropic Messages API
- `ollama/*` → local Ollama daemon
- everything else → generic OpenAI-compat (Groq, Together, vLLM, OpenAI proper)

API keys are read from environment: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `GROQ_API_KEY`, `TOGETHER_API_KEY`. Ollama needs no key.

### What's the EU AI Act angle?
EU AI Act (Q3 2026) requires "documented baselines" for High-Risk AI systems. A signed `indelible.fingerprint.json` is exactly that artefact: a tamper-evident statement of "the agent behaved like *this* on *this* test set on *this* date." The maintainer field carries the attesting party.

### How is this different from Sigstore / sigstore-a2a?
Sigstore signs *code/identity*. The official `sigstore/sigstore-a2a` project (Linux Foundation, 2025–) signs A2A AgentCards — agent *identity*. indelible signs agent *behavior*: the actual statistical fingerprint produced by running the agent against a test suite. Conceptually closer to **in-toto** (attestations *about* artefact properties) than to Sigstore (signing the artefact itself).

### How is this different from Arize Phoenix / WhyLabs / Galileo?
Those are runtime observability platforms — they ingest live traces from your production traffic. indelible runs a fixed test suite offline and produces a signed JSON. Different surfaces:
- runtime + dashboard ↔ CI + signed artefact
- live traffic ↔ fixed test set
- paid SaaS ↔ open-source CLI

Use them together: Phoenix to watch prod, indelible to gate the deploy.

### How is this different from drift-detector?
[drift-detector](https://github.com/MrPredic/drift-detector) is a runtime drift-detection library — it watches behavior live and alerts on shift. indelible defines a **contract** *before* drift happens, signs it, and re-runs it on demand. Sibling projects, complementary surfaces.

### Why is `tool_schema_hash` not numeric like the other signals?
Because it's a SHA-256, not a measurement. Comparing two SHA-256 hashes via numeric tolerance is unsound — close-but-different schemas can land within tolerance and false-pass. So `tool_schema_hash` publishes its full hex in `Signal.digest` and verify does an exact byte-equality check; any tool-schema bit-flip is a breach.

### How big is the fingerprint?
Typical: ~1–2 KB JSON for 7 signals. Signature: 64 bytes. Public key: ~110 bytes PEM. Tiny enough to commit to the repo.

### Is the public key embedded in the fingerprint?
No, it's a companion file (`<fp>.sig.pub`). v0.1 design: explicit and inspectable. v0.2 plans an in-toto envelope where the cert chain travels with the artefact (cosign-style).

## Quickstart with Anthropic (no Ollama needed)

```python
import os, json
from indelible.attest import attest
from indelible.verify import verify
from indelible.config import IndelibleConfig

assert os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"), "set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY first"

config = IndelibleConfig(
    agent_name="quickstart",
    system_prompt="You are a concise, helpful assistant.",
    tools=[],
    model="claude-haiku-4-5",
    provider_url="https://api.anthropic.com",
    maintainer="you@example.com",
)
inputs = [
    "What is 2+2?",
    "Write a Python one-liner to reverse a string.",
    "Name three best practices for error handling.",
]

fp = attest(config, inputs, "claude-haiku-4-5")
with open("indelible.fingerprint.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(fp.to_dict(), f, indent=2)

# ... later, perhaps after a model update ...
report = verify("indelible.fingerprint.json", config, "claude-haiku-4-5", inputs)
print(report.summary())   # "Verification pass — refusal_rate: pass, latency: pass, ..."
assert not report.breached, report.summary()
```

## Quickstart with Ollama (free / offline)

```bash
# Once: pull a model
ollama pull qwen2.5:7b

# Then:
pip install indelible
indelible init
# edit indelible.toml — set model = "ollama/qwen2.5:7b"
indelible attest
indelible verify
```

## Provider exceptions (typed since v0.1)

```python
from indelible.providers import (
    ProviderError,            # base — any HTTP error from a provider
    ProviderAuthError,        # 401/403 — bad/missing API key, do not retry
    ProviderRateLimitError,   # 429 — retry with exponential backoff
    ProviderServerError,      # 5xx — provider outage, retry with backoff
)
```

All four are `RuntimeError` subclasses, so existing `except RuntimeError`
code keeps working — additive change.

## Source

- GitHub: https://github.com/MrPredic/indelible
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/indelible/
- Issues: https://github.com/MrPredic/indelible/issues
- License: MIT

## Prior art / related work

- `sigstore/sigstore-a2a` — Sigstore for A2A AgentCards (signs identity, complementary)
- arXiv 2601.17406 — "Fingerprinting AI Coding Agents on GitHub" (attribution research)
- arXiv 2401.12255 — "Instructional Fingerprinting of LLMs" (model watermarking)
- in-toto / SLSA — generic attestation framework + provenance levels
- Promptfoo, DeepEval — eval frameworks (quality, not behavioral consistency)
- Arize Phoenix, WhyLabs — runtime observability (live traces, not signed offline artefacts)
